name: Mermaid2
description: Improved CFP/YFP FRET variant of Mermaid engineered for larger ratiometric response and submillisecond activation, enabling single-trial detection of action potential trains up to 150 Hz.

spectrum.name: Mermaid2
spectrum.note: Composite FRET spectrum: excitation is the CFP donor (FPbase ECFP, verbatim — corr 1.00; the actual donor is seCFP2, a super-enhanced ECFP variant), emission is the YFP acceptor (FPbase EYFP, verbatim — corr 1.00). Mermaid2 is excited in the CFP band (~435 nm) and read as the YFP/CFP emission ratio. Both arrays normalized to max = 1.0.

voltage.name: Mermaid2
voltage.note: ΔR (YFP/CFP FRET ratio) vs membrane voltage, digitized from the steady-state ΔR–V curve in Fig. 4B (open-circle data markers, which lie on the authors' red Boltzmann fit; Vmid = −32 mV, Zd = 1.05), re-baselined so −70 mV = 0. Ratiometric response, not single-channel ΔF/F. The figure-read −70→+30 step is ≈45%; the paper's text mean for that same step is 48.5 ± 3.5% (n = 6) — the ~3% gap reflects the fit lying slightly below the plateau markers. Re-digitized 2026-07 (overlay-verified); the previous curve was too steep in the −60→0 mV region and did not match Fig. 4B.

kinetics.note: Activation (on) is the amplitude-weighted mean of the double-exponential fit at the +50 mV step (Fig. 4C, annotated on the panel): τfast 0.93 ms (79%), τslow 13.0 ms (21%) → 0.79×0.93 + 0.21×13.0 = 3.46 ms. The paper's headline sub-millisecond activation (0.93 ± 0.21 ms, n = 6, main text) is the fast component alone; storing only that understates the response. Deactivation (off) is the single-exponential decay, 10.3 ms (Fig. 4C). HEK293, ~33°C.

dynamicRangeData.note: Ratiometric ΔR/R (YFP/CFP) per 100 mV (−70→+30 mV): 48.5 ± 3.5% (n = 6, main text), consistent with the steady-state Boltzmann ΔR–V curve in Fig. 4B (Vmid = −32 mV, Zd = 1.05; maximum steady-state response 53.5 ± 2.8% at strong depolarization). FRET ratio change, not single-channel ΔF/F. 1P widefield, HEK293, ~33°C.

sensitivityData.note: Fractional fluorescence change ΔF in the acceptor (YFP) channel per action potential in rat hippocampal neurons: 2.57 ± 0.54% (range 2.1–4.1%, 46 APs in 11 neurons; main text, Fig. 6B). This per-AP value is the single-channel acceptor ΔF/F — NOT the ratiometric ΔR/R used for the 100-mV dynamic range — because the CFP donor channel is too dim to ratio reliably for single spikes (the authors state they analysed the YFP channel signal for spike detection). 1P widefield.

researchPapers.title: Improved detection of electrical activity with a voltage probe based on a voltage-sensing phosphatase
researchPapers.authors: Tsutsui H, Jinno Y, Tomita A, Niino Y, Yamada Y, Mikoshiba K, Miyawaki A, Okamura Y
researchPapers.sample: HEK293 cells, rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro; mouse auditory cortex, in vivo (1P); Xenopus oocytes, in vitro
